summary: A rectangular single-building compound with a large central hall that has stepped gable roofs and flanked by three tall stone towers with flat overhanging caps. The main hall is banded horizontally with light and dark layers and has an open central courtyard or void visible from above. A low decorative perimeter of white blocks outlines the site.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main hall with U-shaped raised roof profile and three separate vertical towers attached near corners/edges; an internal open courtyard/void in the center
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (towers placed roughly symmetrically left/right with a central tower at one end)

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: tower walls and darker horizontal trim

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof edging and horizontal caps (stone slabs)

materials.name: brick
materials.role: possible darker textured layer (could be cobblestone/stonebrick variant)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: not observed prominently but candidate for small vertical details

elements: raised stepped gable roof on the main hall
three tall rectangular towers with small windows and slab-capped flat roofs
horizontal banding on hall walls (light and dark stripes)
external white block perimeter markers outlining footprint
open central courtyard or void inside main hall visible from above
entrance stair/porch feature on one short side
rebuild_hints: Construct a rectangular outer shell with an internal rectangular courtyard leaving a rim of built walls around void (U-shaped roof visible from sides).
Use stacked stairs/half-blocks to create the stepped sloped gable profile on both long faces, mirrored along the long axis.

Add three vertical towers (approximately 4x4 base) rising above the hall: one centered on the rear short side and two near the front corners; use darker stonebrick for tower walls with small 1x1 or 1x2 glass windows at regular vertical intervals.
Apply horizontal banding on the hall walls: repeated pattern of a light white band (likely quartz or white concrete) and darker stone band (stonebrick/cobble) separated by wood planks sections.
Cap towers with overhanging slab roofs (stone slabs) and place a light source (sea lantern or glow block) in the center of each cap.
Outline the site with single-height white block markers spaced evenly to match the photos.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the light white bands: appear to be quartz or white concrete; uncertain which.
Some darker wall textures could be stonebrick vs cobblestone vs blackstone; chosen stonebrick with medium confidence.
Precise interior layout and floor count: images show no clear internal floors except the tower levels; assumed main hall is single-story with tower interiors multi-level.
Exact footprint dimensions and tower placement are estimated from aerials; counts may be off by a few blocks.
Small decorative details (fence, columns, slabs) were inferred in places and may differ in original.
